messages: Considering learning a few basic phrases with a language app — short politeness phrases feel worth it.
Do I need a universal plug adapter? I have so many devices and chargers, but sockets vary.
Might try a small Bluetooth luggage tracker this time — I once had a bag go missing during a layover.
A friend mentioned some neighborhoods have really steep streets; hauling luggage up them was exhausting.
My list of restaurants to try is getting out of hand — might trim it to a handful of musts.
Also booked a dentist appointment the week before we leave; I'm always paranoid about dental issues while traveling.
Okay, could you draft a practical daily plan based on the details I mentioned at the beginning?
Make it suitable for the two travelers, with a few full sightseeing days and some lighter afternoons for downtime.
Include one recommended central place to stay and simple directions from the airport to that neighborhood via public transit or a short taxi ride.
Also provide a per‑day cost estimate (transport, one main meal, and a typical admission) so I can tell if we'll stay within the total we planned.
Flag any attractions where prepaid tickets are strongly advised and note which ones to book in advance.
Because we prefer nonstop travel when reasonable, list a direct flight option each way if available with approximate times and a ballpark cost.
We don't want to drive, so focus on walking, public transit, and short rideshares for transfers and daily movement.
Finally, give one budget‑minded accommodation alternative and one slightly more comfortable option so we can compare tradeoffs.
Keep the plan realistic for a mild spring climate — nothing that assumes extreme heat or freezing cold.
Wrap it up with three short pre‑departure tasks I should definitely handle before leaving.
